Open Paw

Log inDownload Free

Security & Trust

Animal welfare data is sensitive. Adoption applicant information, medical records, donor details, and organizational finances deserve proper protection. Here is how Open Paw handles that responsibility.

Local-first by default

Your data starts on your device. SQLite database, local filesystem. No cloud account required to run Open Paw. Cloud sync is an opt-in feature, not a requirement.

No mandatory cloud

You can run Open Paw indefinitely without creating an account with any cloud provider. Your data stays under your control, on hardware you own or control.

Role-based access control

Volunteers, coordinators, veterinarians, and administrators each have distinct permission levels. Sensitive medical records and financial data are only visible to appropriate roles.

Audit logs

Every write operation is logged with the user, timestamp, and record affected. You can see who changed an animal's status, who updated a medical record, and when.

AI approval gates

AI-assisted features (grant drafts, adoption bios) require explicit human approval before the output is used. No AI action is automatic or irreversible.

No automated medical diagnosis

Open Paw does not diagnose medical conditions. Medical records are logged, not interpreted by AI. Diagnosis remains with licensed veterinary professionals.

What is not in V1

Open Paw V1 is honest about what it does not yet include:

  • —Encrypted at-rest storage (planned for V2)
  • —End-to-end encrypted cloud sync (planned)
  • —Plugin marketplace (not in scope for V1)
  • —Public-input privileged actions (blocked by design)
  • —Automated submission of any external request (blocked by design)

Open source means auditable

The source code for Open Paw is publicly available. Any developer, security researcher, or organization can read the code, verify the security claims, and audit the data handling. There is no "trust us" — there is only the code.

Read the source code on GitHub →
Open Paw

Free, open-source, local-first software for animal rescues, shelters, and welfare teams.

License: AGPL-3.0 — Built for the people doing the work.
